intro: Aleuritia aurantiaca, also known as Bloodroot, is a perennial plant native to North America, belonging to the Papaveraceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating skin conditions, wounds, and oral infections. The bioactive constituents of Bloodroot include isorhapontigenin, benzylisoquinoline alkaloids, and sanguinarine, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
